title: Structured Output Service
description: Build a small but production-minded LLM service that accepts input, returns typed output, handles failure cleanly, and is ready to sit inside a larger product flow.
whyItMatters: Hiring managers increasingly care about typed AI services and system fit, not just chat demos. This project demonstrates that shift.
prerequisites: Complete the Working with LLMs lesson

steps.title: Choose a persona and use case
steps.description: Define your chatbot's purpose: a customer support bot for a specific product, a coding tutor, a recipe assistant, or a travel advisor. Write a detailed persona document.

steps.title: Design the system prompt
steps.description: Write a comprehensive system prompt that defines the persona, rules, output format, and constraints. Include what the bot should and should not do.
steps.tips: Spend real time on this — the system prompt is the most important design decision in any chatbot.

steps.title: Build the API integration
steps.description: Write a Python class that wraps the LLM API. Handle authentication, message formatting, error retries, and rate limiting. Support both Claude and OpenAI APIs.

steps.title: Implement conversation history
steps.description: Store conversation messages and include them in each API call so the bot remembers context. Implement a sliding window to stay within token limits — drop older messages when the context gets too long.

steps.title: Add streaming support
steps.description: Implement streaming responses so the user sees text appear word-by-word instead of waiting for the complete response. This dramatically improves perceived performance.

steps.title: Handle edge cases
steps.description: Test and handle: very long messages, off-topic questions, attempts to override the system prompt, empty messages, API errors, and rate limits. The bot should respond gracefully in every case.

steps.title: Build a simple UI
steps.description: Create a terminal-based chat interface or a simple web UI with Streamlit or Gradio. The user should be able to have a natural conversation.
steps.tips: Streamlit is the fastest path to a web UI: `pip install streamlit` and about 30 lines of code.

deliverables: Working chatbot with defined persona and rules

rubric.description: The chatbot stays in character and follows its defined rules throughout the conversation.
Responses are helpful, contextually aware, and reference previous messages appropriately.
The bot handles off-topic questions, prompt injection attempts, and API errors gracefully.
Clean, well-structured code with proper error handling, type hints, and documentation.
